sources.title: The effect of spermidine on memory performance in older adults at risk for dementia: A randomized controlled trial
Effects of Spermidine Supplementation on Cognition and Biomarkers in Older Adults With Subjective Cognitive Decline: A Randomized Clinical Trial

claims.statement: Spermidine is indexed by PubChem as CID 1102 and associated with CAS number 124-20-9.
PubChem describes spermidine as a polyamine formed from putrescine and found in tissues, supporting its classification as an endogenous polyamine rather than a disease-specific treatment claim.

extractedEvidence.quote: Spermidine is a polyamine formed from putrescine.

reviewFlags: do not translate endogenous mechanism into supplementation outcome

claims.statement: Human studies have investigated spermidine-rich supplementation in older adults with subjective cognitive decline or dementia-risk contexts, but BioStack should treat cognition claims as emerging and study-context-specific.

context.population: older adults with subjective cognitive decline or dementia-risk contexts
context.formulation: spermidine-rich plant or wheat-germ extract supplements

extractedEvidence.quote: This is the first human study to evaluate the impact of spermidine supplementation on memory performance in older adults

reviewFlags: cognition evidence is emerging and should not be generalized

claims.statement: BioStack should separate spermidine autophagy/longevity marketing claims from human clinical outcomes; current pilot evidence supports tracking the research area, not presenting broad anti-aging conclusions.
reviewFlags: vendor longevity claims require misinformation monitoring

reviewReasons: Pilot emerging evidence packet requires human review before publication.
